course_info.title: Business Capstone Project
course_info.meeting_schedule: Wednesday - 2 hours seminar + independent project work

learning_objectives: Understand capstone project requirements and expectations
Identify a real business problem suitable for capstone analysis
Form project teams and establish team charters
readings: Sample Capstone Projects from Prior Years

lecture_notes.introduction: The Business Capstone is the culminating experience of the BSBA program. You will apply everything you have learned to solve a real business problem for a real organization.
key_concepts: Capstone as integrative experience across all business functions
Project selection criteria: real problem, accessible data, measurable outcomes
Team formation: complementary skills and interests
Project charter: scope, objectives, timeline, deliverables
Faculty advisor and industry sponsor roles
lecture_notes.demonstration: Review of three exemplary capstone projects from prior years, analyzing what made them successful

review.value: Demonstrates mastery of business knowledge by applying strategy, finance, marketing, operations, and analytics to a real problem with actionable recommendations
The best capstone projects solve a real problem for a real organization with access to real data; avoid hypothetical scenarios

class_schedule.topic: Research Methodology and Data Collection Planning
learning_objectives: Design a research methodology appropriate to the business problem
Plan primary and secondary data collection strategies
Address ethical considerations in business research

lecture_notes.introduction: Rigorous methodology separates a capstone project from a casual opinion piece. Your analysis must be grounded in systematically collected and analyzed data.
key_concepts: Qualitative methods: interviews, focus groups, observation
Quantitative methods: surveys, financial analysis, analytics
Mixed methods: combining qualitative insight with quantitative rigor
Data collection planning: what data, from whom, how, when
Research ethics: informed consent, confidentiality, IRB requirements
lecture_notes.demonstration: Developing a data collection plan for a capstone project: identifying data needs, sources, methods, and timeline

review.value: Combining qualitative (why) and quantitative (how much) approaches provides both depth of understanding and statistical rigor
All human subjects research requires informed consent, data protection, and may require IRB review; plan for this early

class_schedule.topic: Milestone 1 Workshop: Problem Definition and Literature Review
learning_objectives: Present refined problem statements for peer and faculty feedback
Demonstrate understanding of existing research and industry context
Receive structured feedback to improve project direction

lecture_notes.introduction: This workshop provides structured feedback on your problem definition and initial research. Use this feedback to sharpen your focus before investing heavily in data collection.
key_concepts: Problem statement refinement: specific, measurable, bounded
Literature review: academic research, industry reports, case studies
Stakeholder input integration
Feedback incorporation process
Revised project timeline based on scoping
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents their problem definition and receives 10 minutes of structured feedback from faculty and peers

review.value: A strong problem statement is specific enough to be researchable, important enough to matter to the sponsor, and bounded enough to be completed in one semester
Establishes what is already known, identifies gaps your project will fill, and grounds your methodology in established approaches

class_schedule.topic: Milestone 2 Workshop: Data Collection Progress and Preliminary Analysis
learning_objectives: Report on data collection progress and challenges
Present preliminary data analysis and emerging patterns
Adjust methodology based on data availability and quality

lecture_notes.introduction: At this stage, you should have significant data collected and initial analysis underway. This workshop addresses data quality issues and helps refine your analytical approach.
key_concepts: Data quality assessment and cleaning strategies
Preliminary pattern identification
Methodology adjustments based on actual data
Progress tracking against project timeline
Collaboration with industry sponsor on interim findings
lecture_notes.demonstration: Each team presents data collection status, preliminary findings, and any methodology pivots needed

review.value: Assess completeness, accuracy, and relevance of collected data; better to identify issues now and adjust than to build analysis on flawed data
If data availability differs from plan, adapt the methodology while maintaining rigor; document and justify any changes

class_schedule.topic: Milestone 3 Workshop: Analysis and Recommendations Development
learning_objectives: Present completed analysis with supporting evidence
Develop actionable recommendations from analytical findings
Prepare implementation roadmaps with financial projections

lecture_notes.introduction: Analysis without actionable recommendations is academic exercise, not business consulting. Your recommendations must be specific, feasible, and supported by your data.
key_concepts: From analysis to insight: identifying the 'so what'
Recommendation development: specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, time-bound
Implementation planning: phased approach with quick wins
Financial impact estimation: ROI, payback, risk-adjusted projections
Recommendation prioritization matrix
lecture_notes.demonstration: Workshop on translating analytical findings into a prioritized recommendation set with implementation roadmap

review.value: Each recommendation must answer: what specifically to do, who is responsible, when to start, what it will cost, and what the expected benefit is
Identify low-effort, high-impact recommendations that can be implemented immediately to build momentum and credibility for larger changes

class_schedule.topic: Milestone 4/5: Final Presentations and Report Delivery
learning_objectives: Deliver professional presentations to industry sponsors and faculty panel
Submit final capstone report meeting professional consulting standards
Reflect on the capstone experience and key learnings

lecture_notes.introduction: The final presentation is your opportunity to demonstrate the full breadth of your business education. Present with the confidence and professionalism of management consultants delivering to a client.
key_concepts: Executive presentation skills: confidence, clarity, storytelling
Q&A handling: bridging, acknowledging limitations, redirecting
Report structure: executive summary, methodology, analysis, recommendations, appendices
Client deliverable standards: professional formatting, data visualization, actionable content
Reflection and lessons learned
lecture_notes.demonstration: Final team presentations (20 minutes each) to a panel of faculty and industry sponsors, followed by Q&A

review.value: The report and presentation must be of a quality that the industry sponsor can immediately use to inform decisions and present to their leadership
20 minutes: 15 for presentation, 5 for Q&A. Every team member speaks. Dress professionally. Know your material cold.

major_assignments.title: Milestone 1: Project Proposal and Literature Review
major_assignments.description: Submit a formal project proposal including problem statement, research questions, methodology plan, and literature review establishing the context for your project.
objectives: Define a clear, bounded business problem

criteria: Clear problem definition with business significance

major_assignments.title: Milestone 2: Data Collection and Preliminary Analysis
major_assignments.description: Report on data collection progress, present preliminary analysis, and document any methodology adjustments.

major_assignments.title: Milestone 3: Draft Report and Recommendations
major_assignments.description: Submit a draft final report including complete analysis, recommendations with implementation plan, and financial impact assessment.

major_assignments.title: Milestone 4: Final Presentation
major_assignments.description: Deliver a 20-minute professional presentation to the faculty panel and industry sponsor, demonstrating mastery of analysis and recommendations.
objectives: Present findings and recommendations with executive-level clarity
Demonstrate deep understanding of the business problem and analysis
Handle Q&A with confidence and expertise

criteria: Clear communication of findings and recommendations

major_assignments.title: Milestone 5: Final Report
major_assignments.description: Submit the final polished capstone report incorporating all feedback from milestones and presentation, ready for delivery to the industry sponsor.
objectives: Incorporate all feedback from prior milestones and presentation
Ensure all analysis is rigorous and recommendations are actionable
Produce a document the sponsor can use immediately
deliverables: All supporting data and analysis in appendices
Reflection essay on lessons learned (individual, 2 pages)

criteria: Executive summary captures everything a busy executive needs

peer_evaluation.description: Team member contributions assessed by peers

individual_reflection.description: Individual reflection essay on capstone experience

course_policies.attendance: Mandatory for all workshop sessions and milestone presentations. Industry sponsor meetings are scheduled by teams.
course_policies.late_submissions: Milestone submissions have firm deadlines. Late milestones penalized 15% per day as they affect the entire team.
course_policies.collaboration: Capstone is a team project (3-4 members). All team members must contribute substantively. Peer evaluations are confidential.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All data must be authentically collected. Industry sponsor data is confidential and subject to NDA. Plagiarism results in course failure for all involved.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Analytics tools (Python/R, Tableau), presentation software, word processing, and collaboration tools (shared drives, project management software).
